text.title: Project Integration Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the project-integration methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: One YAML file (integrated/plan.yaml) in the project repo is the single source of truth; derivatives (slides, PDFs, dashboards) are generated, never hand-edited.
rule.rationale: Manual edits diverge from the source within one week; agents pulling from wrong derivatives produce misleading status reports.

rule.statement: Charter.md lives in the project repo; sponsor approves via PR review with signed commit. Amendments require a new PR, not a stealth edit.
rule.rationale: Git history of charter changes is the audit trail for change authorization.

rule.statement: Every approved CR requires a cross-area impact memo committed alongside the CR before implementation begins.
rule.rationale: Second-order effects (a 2-week delay shifting onto a holiday, breaking a vendor deadline) surface only in cross-area analysis.

rule.statement: Status colour is derived from numeric thresholds, never PM narrative. The threshold table is part of the plan.
rule.rationale: Narrative status is subjective and unreliable; threshold-driven status is deterministic and auditable.

rule.statement: Re-baseline only when scope/budget/contract changes — not when velocity slips. Re-baselining for slip masks chronic delivery problems.
rule.rationale: Baseline changes are decisions, not corrections; treating slip as a reason to re-baseline hides the root cause and makes EVM meaningless.

rule.statement: One decisions/ directory per project, one ADR-style entry per approved CR with rationale + impact + signature.
rule.rationale: Decision rationale lost verbally; documented decisions become searchable corporate memory.

list.item: Single-team, single-stream work with a stable backlog — Scrum or Kanban already integrates within the team
Short tactical engagement under 4 weeks with no inter-area trade-offs
Pure research / discovery phase with no committed scope
